title: cansjera polystachya
intro: Cansjera polystachya, also known as the "Chain Fruit Vine" or "Kalanasa", is a perennial climber that belongs to the family Opiliaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cansjera polystachya include the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of decoctions or infusions, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
